forecasts.reasoning: The base rate for signing the Artemis Accords has been roughly one country per month on average, with 18 countries joining in 2024 alone. Given this base rate, the probability of at least one country signing in any given month is typically around 50-60%. Furthermore, a NASA press release from May 22, 2025, states that "NASA anticipates additional countries will sign in the coming weeks and months," suggesting active negotiations are nearing completion. Most importantly, the 68th session of the UN Committee on the Peaceful Uses of Outer Space (COPUOS) is scheduled from June 25 to July 4, 2025. Historically, Artemis Accords signings are heavily clustered around high-profile international space conferences like this one. While I do not have names of specific countries slated to sign in June, the convergence of the base rate, recent statements by NASA, and the optimal platform provided by COPUOS in late June strongly suggest a highly elevated chance of a signing event during the June 1–June 30 timeframe.

The forecast horizon is extremely short (approx. 33 days between the knowledge cutoff of May 29, 2025, and the July 1, 2025, deadline). While Senator Fetterman is facing significant political and personal blowback following a scathing New York Magazine profile in early May, his response has been wholly defiant. He has called the article a "hit piece," explicitly stated he is in "good health," and declared he will not resign or switch parties, intending to serve his full term until 2029. Furthermore, despite reports of him leaving a Democratic caucus group chat and being courted by Republicans, Democratic leadership such as Chuck Schumer continue to defend him publicly as a "good legislator," indicating no formal party mechanism is mobilizing to expel him quickly. Given the very high bar and typically lengthy timeline for a sitting Senator to resign or change caucuses—especially when actively resisting such calls—the only plausible path to a 'Yes' resolution within 33 days is a sudden, extreme deterioration in his health that forces an immediate departure. This represents a very small tail risk.
